changes: Added `power` module import to dispatcher dependencies
Created `pause_all_running()` stub function with TODO comment for deferred implementation
Spawned power state watcher on dedicated OS thread via `power::spawn_power_watcher()`
Added new select arm in dispatcher main loop: `Ok(()) = power_rx.changed()` handler
Implemented power state machine: Sleeping → pause cards, Awake → rescan and resume
file_insights.impact: Core dispatcher loop now responds to OS power state changes

patterns_discovered.description: Power watcher runs on dedicated OS thread (blocking I/O), communicates with async dispatcher via tokio::sync::watch channel
patterns_discovered.evidence: power::spawn_power_watcher() returns watch receiver; dispatcher uses power_rx.changed() and power_rx.borrow_and_update()

patterns_discovered.description: Deferred implementation pattern: pause_all_running() is a no-op stub that logs intent, actual logic marked TODO for future subtask
patterns_discovered.evidence: Function body contains eprintln! and TODO comment

patterns_discovered.description: System wake event triggers needs_scan = true to force card re-dispatch, ensuring no stale state
patterns_discovered.evidence: Both Sleeping and Awake branches manage needs_scan state

patterns_discovered.pattern: Non-Fatal Error Handling in Event Loop
patterns_discovered.description: Power state changes logged as warnings, do not halt dispatcher
patterns_discovered.evidence: if let Err(e) = pause_all_running() { eprintln!(...) } - error logged but loop continues

gotchas_discovered.note: Stub function currently does nothing. Will need to define: pause semantics, card lifecycle during pause, resume behavior

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Power state transitions on OS thread, dispatcher on async task
gotchas_discovered.note: Correct architecture but requires careful synchronization via channel; no blocking I/O in dispatcher loop

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No check for concurrent pause_all_running invocations
gotchas_discovered.note: If power watcher emits multiple Sleeping events, pause_all_running could be called repeatedly before Awake
No specification of where paused cards are stored, how they're moved/tracked, or what triggers actual resume

approach_details.strategy: Minimal integration of power watcher into dispatcher event loop with deferred implementation of pause logic
approach_details.execution: Clean, idiomatic tokio integration. Power watcher spawned early in dispatcher startup, integrated into select! macro

recommendations.recommendation: Define pause_all_running() actual logic: enumerate running cards, transition them to paused state, ensure they don't get moved to failed/ on system wake
Design paused card lifecycle: introduce 'paused' intermediate state or use existing state with marker file, prevent merge-gate/policy-check from treating paused cards as stuck
Ensure pause_all_running() is idempotent (safe to call multiple times) and handles case where no cards are running
Add power state transition test: mock power watcher, verify dispatcher calls pause_all_running() on Sleeping, verify needs_scan set on Awake
Consider structured logging (trace level) for power state changes to aid debugging of sleep/wake issues
